C00.summary: Product/harness boundaries hold. hermes/project.json marks the repo as product_codebase with harness_role=validation-only; autonomous_agent_policy forbids production access/write and every validation_command sets writes_product_state=false. Mutual-agreement contract keeps human approval non-delegable and blocks completion-from-planning. validate-goal1-foundation.mjs gates closeout on the real review result verdicts and live package files, not packet existence. No AI/Hermes/Claude production-mutation path found.

C01.summary: Tenant identity is consistent (every entity except Tenant requires tenant_id). Matter cannot be created without client_id + owner_user_id; createDocumentReference holds only a reference and forces dms_owned=true; assertSameTenant/assertMatterBelongsToClient reject cross-tenant links before permission logic, with a passing test. One P3: User.email is stored with no PII classification while Client/Matter carry confidentiality.

C02.summary: Evaluator order matches the contract decision_order: cross_tenant_deny → deny_rule → object_acl_deny → review/approval → object_acl_allow → allow → fail_closed_no_match. Cross-tenant fails closed with no admin exception; deny precedes all allows; review/approval override an allow ACL (tested); trimSearchResults derives counts from the post-trim allowed set. P3: abac and rbac are collapsed into one ordered allow step (contract-acknowledged deferral) and the ethical_wall_matter_id match path has no dedicated test.

C03.summary: Events are deep-frozen; corrections are appended (action=audit.correction.append) not mutated. verifyHashChain detects sequence breaks, previous_hash/event_hash mismatch, clock skew, hash-algorithm mismatch and orphaned corrections; chains and reads are tenant-scoped with tenant-scoped idempotency. canPurgeAuditEvent returns legal_hold_blocks_purge before any other check. P3s: missing vs duplicate sequence both collapse to reason 'sequence_gap' (contract enumerates them separately), and forbidden-payload/masking policy is enforced only structurally (no raw-body field) with no active content scan.

findings.title: User PII (email) carried without classification
findings.details: createUser stores email in plaintext with no PII/confidentiality classification, while createClient and createMatter validate a confidentiality level. The C01 packet explicitly lists 'PII classification gaps' as a must-challenge risk.
findings.recommendation: Before downstream modules (DMS/Billing/AI) consume User identity, add a PII classification/handling marker for email (and future personal fields) or document the deferral in the core-domain contract.

findings.title: ABAC/RBAC collapsed into a single allow step; ethical-wall-by-matter path untested
findings.details: decision_order in permission-kernel-contract.json lists abac and rbac as distinct steps, but evaluate.js represents both as one ordered allow-rule scan. The contract precedence_notes already flag this as 'must be separated before RP02 production_ready'. Separately, matches() supports ethical_wall_matter_id but the test suite only exercises ethical wall via a generic deny rule, not the ethical_wall_matter_id match.
findings.recommendation: Track the abac/rbac separation as a concrete RP02 correction subphase and add a test that denies via ethical_wall_matter_id matching resource.matter_id.

findings.title: Hash-chain gap reasons coarser than contract taxonomy
findings.details: audit-compliance-contract immutability.gap_detection enumerates missing_sequence_number and duplicate_sequence_number distinctly, but verifyHashChain reports both as reason 'sequence_gap'. Detection is correct; only the emitted reason code is less specific than the contract enumerates.
findings.recommendation: Distinguish missing vs duplicate sequence in the returned reason to match the contract's gap_detection taxonomy.

findings.title: Payload privacy policy enforced structurally, not by content scan
findings.details: createAuditEvent accepts only payload_digest/payload_classification and exposes no raw-body field, so forbidden values cannot be logged through this API. However, privacy_and_payload_policy.forbidden_payload_values and masking_required_for are not actively validated; a caller embedding PII inside evidence_refs or object fields would not be caught.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit payload/evidence-shape guard and a fixture asserting forbidden values are rejected before RP03 production_ready.

evidence_commands_run: Read contracts/law-firm-os.product-contract.json, core-domain-contract.json, permission-kernel-contract.json, audit-compliance-contract.json, goal1-mutual-agreement-contract.json, goal1-claude-review-contract.json
Glob apps/**/package.json, packages/*/package.json; Grep weighted-implementation-ledger.json for subphase/requirement/template counts
No Bash/shell commands executed (per review constraints)
parsed_review.review_notes: Read-only review of the actual referenced files, not packet presence. The four security-critical properties for this slice are genuinely implemented and covered by passing node:test assertions: deny-over-allow with object-ACL denies (authz.test.js), cross-tenant fail-closed even without an admin exception, append-only deep-frozen events with tamper/gap/clock-skew/orphan-correction detection, and legal-hold-blocks-purge ordered ahead of every other purge gate. Boundary controls (no AI/Hermes/Claude production mutation; human approval non-delegable; completion gated on real evidence) are intact. All findings are P3 and forward-looking; none block Goal 1 closeout. One non-finding observation on C00: validate-goal1-foundation.mjs asserts fixed planning-ledger magic numbers (54,355 subphases / 227 requirements, both confirmed present) as a closeout gate — this is artifact-shape checking rather than product verification, but it is paired with real package tests so it is not validation theater. Frontend stack note: product contract names Next.js/React as a candidate while apps/web is Vite-based — not a violation since the contract field is explicitly 'candidate'.
